title: anti_anchor (v1)
description: A registered or refined forward false-anchor. Mirrors and extends the existing techne/registry/anti_anchors.jsonl shape. Tier-1 mandatory; all other tiers optional. Field-level HARD-5 + anti-leakage enforcement: false_form/true_form minimum lengths, citation regex matching, withdrawn-status structured patterns.

false_form.title: Forward false statement an LLM might emit
false_form.description: One-sentence false statement, written in the voice of a hypothetical LLM emission. The substrate's anti-anchor catches this exact false form mechanically.

true_form.title: True statement with all conditional qualifiers
true_form.description: One-paragraph true statement. ALL conditional qualifiers (regime restrictions, special-case carve-outs, definitional qualifiers like 'occurrence vs multiplicity') MUST appear here verbatim. Minimum length 60 to discourage qualifier-clipping; the substrate-tester pattern shows clipped qualifiers are the dominant anti-anchor failure mode.

citation.description: Primary source. Not 'BIP 2019' alone — must be the arXiv ID or DOI. Hallucinated arXiv IDs fail at parse time via the regex; the verify_arxiv_citations.py post-step adds existence + withdrawal checks.

verified_against_primary.title: Was the primary source actually consulted?
verified_against_primary.description: Boolean assertion the reviewer must re-verify. The validator records the assertion; it does not trust it. Required true for Tier-1 verifications.

last_verified.description: Most recent verification date. Anti-anchors get re-verified every 90-120 days.

recommended_action.title: What ingestion should do with this block

refines_existing_aa_id.description: When recommended_action is 'refine-existing' or 'invert-existing', the AA-NNN being refined/inverted. Cross-reference checked at validate-time.

risk_tier.title: How load-bearing is this anti-anchor
risk_tier.description: high = active false-anchor likely to be emitted by current LLMs; medium = plausible false-anchor in the literature; low = niche or already well-corrected.

examples.name: GCT_OCCURRENCE_DEAD
examples.false_form: Bürgisser-Ikenmeyer-Panova killed GCT entirely; occurrence obstructions for det/padded-perm are still a viable path to VP vs VNP separation.
examples.true_form: BIP 2019 (J. AMS) killed *occurrence* obstructions for (det_m, padded_perm_{n,m}, m=poly(n)) specifically. Multiplicity, vanishing-ideal, outside-orbit, and equivariant obstructions remain. Substrate must reject any agent attempt to construct an OccurrenceObstruction in this regime as a sentinel-violation.
